ORDER DENYING ANDRES MURGUIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL ALLSTATE TO FURTHER RESPOND TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANDRES MURGUIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL ALLSTATE TO FURTHER RESPOND TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMETNS, SET ONE IS DENIED. OF THE SIX CATEGORIES IN THE ORIGINAL MOTION, ONLY TWO REMAIN IN DISPUTE: (1) DEFENDANT'S MARKETING AND ADVERTISING MATERIALS (REQUESTS NOS. 5-7) AND (2) LEGAL COMPLAINTS AND VERDICTS OR JUDGMENTS AGAINST DEFENDANT (REQUESTS NOS. 14 AND 15). FOR REQUEST NOS. 5 - 7, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS PERTAINING TO DEFENDANT'S REPRESENTATIONS OR ADVERTISEMENTS IS REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. (CCP 2031.310(B)(1).) THIS ACTION IS LIMITED TO THE HANDLING OF ONE PARTICULAR CLAIM UNDER ON PARTICULAR POLICY, AND NOT FALSE ADVERTISING. FOR REQUEST NOS. 14 - 15, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS PERTAINING TO LEGAL COMPLAINTS AND VERDICTS OR JUDGMENTS AGAINST DEFENDANT IS REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. THE INSTANT DISCOVERY IS DISTINGUISHABLE FROM COLONIAL LIFE. (COLONIAL LIFE & ACCIDENT INS. CO. V. SUP.CT. (1982) 31 CAL.3D 785. IN COLONIAL LIFE, THE DISCOVERY AT ISSUE WAS THE IDENTITIES OF 35 OTHER CLAIMANTS WHO COULD BE CALLED AS POTENTIAL WITNESSES IN SUPPORT OF A "PATTERN OR PRACTICE" THEORY OF LIABILITY. IN CONTRAST, REQUEST NOS. 14 - 15 SEEK DISCOVERY OF COMPLAINTS AND VERDICTS/JUDGMENTS FROM OTHER LAWSUITS, WHICH MAY OR MAY NOT IDENTIFY POTENTIAL WITNESSES OR RELEVANT FACTS. FURTHERMORE, REQUEST NOS. 14 - 15 ARE OVERBROAD. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-08-2014 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-23-2013 Set For Trial On Oct-06-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Allstate To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documetns, Set One.
Off Calendar Nov-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ANDRES MURGUIA'S Motion To Compel Allstate To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documetns, Set One is Denied. Of the six categories in the original motion, only two remain in dispute: (1) Defendant's marketing and advertising materials (Requests Nos. 5-7) and (2) legal complaints and verdicts or judgments against Defendant (Requests Nos. 14 and 15). For Request nos. 5 - 7, Plaintiff has not established that production of documents pertaining to Defendant's representations or advertisements is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2031.310(b)(1).) This action is limited to the handling of one particular claim under on particular policy, and not false advertising. For Request nos. 14 - 15, Plaintiff has not established that production of documents pertaining to legal complaints and verdicts or judgments against Defendant is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The instant discovery is distinguishable from Colonial Life. (Colonial Life & Accident Ins. Co. v. Sup.Ct. (1982) 31 Cal.3d 785. In Colonial Life, the discovery at issue was the identities of 35 other claimants who could be called as potential witnesses in support of a "pattern or practice" theory of liability. In contrast, Request nos. 14 - 15 seek discovery of complaints and verdicts/judgments from other lawsuits, which may or may not identify potential witnesses or relevant facts. Furthermore, Request nos. 14 - 15 are overbroad. Monetary sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Allstate To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documetns, Set One.
Off Calendar Oct-30-2013 Continued To Nov-13-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on Wednesday, October 30, 2013:
Whitney Barnecut, Esq. of Hereford Kerley LLP [510.379.5801] appearing for Plaintiff ANDRES MURGUIA;
Michelle A. Bradley, Esq. of Dentons US LLP [415.882.0300] appearing for Defendant ALLSTATE INSURANCE COMPANY

The parties do not stipulate to have this matter heard by the judge pro tem. The matter is argued. Plaintiff ANDRES MURGUIA'S Motion To Compel Allstate To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One is continued to November 13, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. Judge Pro Tem: Anthony Schoenberg; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
